name: Coinstar Kiosk
description_short: Coinstar operates self-service kiosks that convert coins to cash, eGift cards, and enable cryptocurrency purchases and cash transfers at retail locations.
description_long:
Coinstar has established itself as a network of self-service kiosks located primarily in grocery stores and retail environments across the United States. The company provides convenient cash conversion and financial services without requiring customers to visit traditional banks or financial institutions. Founded on the core service of converting loose change into usable currency, Coinstar has expanded significantly into adjacent financial services. The company is currently owned by Arctic Slope Regional Corporation (ASRC) as of February 2026.

At the PayOMatic kiosk location in Long Island City, NY, Coinstar offers a diverse range of services beyond basic coin conversion. Primary offerings include Coins to Cash conversion with variable fees by location, eGift card exchanges with no-fee options available, and charity donations through the Coins that Count program. The kiosk also facilitates cryptocurrency purchases through partnerships with CINQ by Coinstar (powered by Zero Hash) and Coinme, allowing customers to convert cash and coins into 25+ cryptocurrency forms. Additional services include Amazon Cash account loading, account transfers to checking accounts via partner financial institutions, and the CINQ by Coinstar debit card.

Coinstar distinguishes itself through multi-service kiosk integration, eliminating the need for customers to visit multiple locations for different financial transactions. The company has built strategic partnerships with major retailers (Amazon), cryptocurrency platforms (Coinme, Zero Hash), and financial institutions for account transfers. Their "no fee" eGift card options and charity donation program provide value-conscious alternatives to traditional coin conversion. The emphasis on digital wallet integration (requiring app downloads for crypto services) reflects modernization of legacy coin-exchange services.

However, Coinstar's service scope creates limitations. The kiosk explicitly does not exchange gift cards or foreign currency, restricting utility for certain customer needs. Cryptocurrency purchasing requires app setup and account verification before visiting the kiosk, adding friction to the transaction process. While "fees may vary by location," the website does not disclose specific fee amounts, creating pricing opacity. The Long Island City location specifically notes it does not accept bills, limiting cash-related services at this particular kiosk. These limitations suggest Coinstar functions as a supplementary financial access point rather than a comprehensive banking alternative.
pros: Coins to Cash conversion available in minutes without sorting, counting, or rolling coins
Multiple no-fee eGift card options available, reducing transaction costs
Charity donation integration allows direct charitable giving from spare change
Cryptocurrency purchasing available at kiosks through Coinme and CINQ partnerships
Amazon Cash service enables account loading without credit or debit cards
Account transfer service to checking accounts via partner financial institutions
Convenient retail locations (grocery stores) for 24/7 access in many markets
CINQ debit card option provides digital wallet and privacy-focused financial management
cons: Coin-to-cash fees vary by location with no transparent pricing disclosed on website
Cryptocurrency services require app downloads and account setup before kiosk visit, adding friction
This specific Long Island City location does not accept bills, limiting utility
Company explicitly does not exchange foreign currency or gift cards, restricting service scope
Account transfer fees vary by financial institution partner with no upfront disclosure
best_for: Consumers with accumulated loose change seeking quick cash conversion at retail locations
Amazon shoppers wanting to load account balance without credit or debit cards
Cryptocurrency-curious customers seeking accessible entry points for digital asset purchases
Charitable donors who want to convert spare change into donations to supported organizations
diagnosis: Coinstar kiosks serve consumers seeking convenient cash access and financial services at retail locations, particularly those with accumulated coins or those buying cryptocurrency. The main caveat is that pricing is location-dependent and non-transparent, cryptocurrency services require app friction, and this specific Long Island City location does not accept bills despite being branded as offering cash services.
services: Coins to Cash conversion with variable location-based fees
eGift card exchanges (no-fee options available across multiple retailers)
Coins that Count charitable donation program
CINQ by Coinstar cryptocurrency conversion (25+ cryptocurrency options)
Amazon Cash account loading ($5-$500 per transaction)
Account transfer to checking accounts via financial institution partners
CINQ by Coinstar debit card with digital wallet functionality
